section.heading: Incorporation by Reference.
section.text: In this chapter, the following documents are incorporated by reference to the extent that they are referenced in this chapter:

para.text: The ASTM International Standards on Amusement Rides and Devices: F2374-22 Standard Practice for Design, Manufacture, Operation, and Maintenance of Inflatable Amusement Devices (2022) (ASTM F2374-22), excluding Sections 5.5.5.2(4), 5.12.7.2(2), 5.16.6, 7.5.4.1 reference to 7.9, 7.5.5.3(2), 7.7.4, 7.9, 7.10 & 7.11.1; and
The National Electrical Code, ANSI/NFPA #70-2020, which is incorporated by reference in COMAR 20.50.02.02.
In addition to terms defined in Business Regulation Article, §3-101, Annotated Code of Maryland, in this chapter the following terms have the meanings indicated.
Terms Defined.
“ASTM” means ASTM International, originally known as the American Society for Testing and Materials.
“Attendant” means a person assigned to assist the operator in attending to the safety needs of users.
“Certificate of inspection of an inflatable amusement attraction” means a certificate issued by the Commissioner of Labor and Industry.
“Exit means a doorway or other opening affording safe and unobstructed egress.
“Fire official” means the authority having jurisdiction in the locality over matters relating to fire prevention and explosion.
“Inflatable amusement attraction” means an air-supported amusement attraction that:
Incorporates a structural and mechanical system; and
Uses a high strength fabric or film that achieves its strength, shape, and stability by tensioning from internal air pressure.

“Inflatable Amusement Attraction Daily Inspection and Daily Pre-Opening Checklist” means the Commissioner of Labor and Industry’s form to be completed by the owner prior to the operation of an inflatable amusement attraction.

“Operator” means a person who is trained and who is capable of identifying existing and predictable hazards involving the inflatable amusement attraction operation, and has the authority to control and eliminate any hazards.
“Owner” means a person, the State or a political subdivision of the State that owns an inflatable amusement attraction, or if the amusement attraction is leased, the lessee.
“Qualified person” means a person who has successfully demonstrated the ability to solve or resolve problems related to the subject matter or the work:
By possession of a recognized degree, certificate, or professional standing; or
By extensive knowledge, training, and experience.
“Permanently erected inflatable amusement attraction” means an inflatable amusement attraction erected in the same location for more than 30 consecutive days.
“Rated capacity” means a capacity:
Established by the manufacturer for the normal loading and operation of an inflatable amusement attraction by weight or number of users; or
If the manufacturer has not established a capacity, as established by the Commissioner after inspection.
“Safety work order” means a written demand issued by an authorized representative of the Commissioner to perform work that has potential safety impact if not corrected.

“Special inflated amusement structure” means a fun house, dark ride, walk through, haunted house, or any other similar inflated amusement attraction that uses air pressure to support the walls and roof of the structure.
“Sustained wind speed” means wind speed determined by averaging observed values over a two-minute period.
User.
“User” means any person who is a participant in the use of an inflatable amusement attraction as defined by the manufacturer’s specifications.
“User” does not include employees of the inflatable amusement attraction owner while engaged in the duties of their employment.
“Wind gust” means a rapid fluctuation of wind speed determined by averaging observed values over a two-minute period.
For an inflatable amusement attraction manufactured after January 1, 2026, in addition to the requirements of this chapter, the requirements of ASTM F2374-22 shall apply.
For an inflatable amusement attraction manufactured before January 1, 2026, in addition to the requirements of this chapter, ASTM F2374-22 shall apply except Sections 5.5.5, 5.5.5.1, 5.13, and 6.1.3.
Each owner shall obtain the manufacturer’s operations manual and shall:
Read and become familiar with the contents of the manufacturer’s recommended operating instructions and specifications;
Keep the manufacturer’s operations manual on location with the inflatable amusement attraction; and
Provide a copy of the manufacturer’s operations manual, in English, to the Commissioner on request.
The owner shall set up and operate an inflatable amusement attraction in accordance with the following:
Owner requirements for setting up inflatables amusement attractions.
An inflatable amusement attraction shall not be operated when the maximum sustained wind speed is 15 mph or greater; or
When there are wind gusts lasting 3 seconds or longer with wind speed of 25 mph or greater in the immediate area where the inflatable attraction is being operated.
Anchorage points installed near an entrance or exit of an inflatable amusement attraction shall be connected in such a manner as to minimize the potential for tripping, abrasions, or other injuries;
Blowers and inflation tubes shall be positioned to prevent kinks or bends in the inflation tube; and
Inflatable amusement attractions shall be anchored or secured in accordance with the manufacturer’s specifications or the following requirements:
The on-ground anchor weight used for inflatable amusement attractions shall be at least 75 pounds for bounce-type rides;
The on-ground anchor weight for slide-type rides over 15 feet tall shall be at least 500 pounds for each installed anchor position;
Anchors shall be used at all of the manufacturer’s required positions at all times for both indoor and outdoor use;
Each anchor must be:
At least 1 inch in diameter; and
Solid in composition;
Anchors can be straight stakes, screw stakes, ground weights, or sandbag ground anchors, and must comply with the following:
If straight stakes are used, they must be at least 36 inches in length with at least 75 percent or more of the length in the ground; and
The ends of the stakes should be covered to prevent a tripping hazard;
Tie-downs should be attached to a secure device or permanent structure and attached so that they cannot slip off the anchor during use;
Tie-downs should not be attached to motor vehicles;
Tie-downs should comply with manufacturer’s specifications or have a minimum tensile strength of 3,700 pounds or 370 pounds safe working load; and
Permanently erected inflatable amusement attractions shall comply with the following:
The manufacturer’s specifications;
The requirements of ASTM F2374-22.5.6.9.1;
The requirements in §B(1)—(4) of this regulation; and
If erected with stakes or bolts, the stakes or bolts shall be inspected prior to each operation to ensure:
Secure anchorage;
No corrosion of anchorage; and
No debris near anchorage.
Weather conditions and forecast for the zip code area where the inflatable amusement attraction is operated shall be:
Set-up monitoring.
Monitored prior to set up of the inflatable amusement attraction; and
Recorded in a written log as provided for in Regulation .12(D)(1)(b) of this regulation;
Monitored periodically during operations using a National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration weather radio application or source similar in reliability.
Wind speeds shall be monitored prior to the set up of an inflatable amusement attraction and periodically during operation using an anemometer or other reliable method.
Operator, Attendant, and User Operating Requirements.
For an inflatable amusement attraction less than 15 feet in height, the owner shall provide the appropriate number of operators and attendants as specified by the manufacturer.

For an inflatable amusement attraction over 15 feet in height, the owner shall follow the manufacturer’s specifications as to the appropriate number of operators and attendants, but a minimum of one operator and one attendant shall be provided.
For an inflatable amusement attraction over 15 feet in height, the owner shall follow the manufacturer’s specifications as to maximum number of users.
If the manufacturer’s specifications do not provide the number of users, there shall be no more than two users on the attraction and two users in a queue at any one time.
General Owner Obligations.
An owner shall comply with ASTM F2374-22.7.4.
The owner shall ensure that the amusement attraction is positioned at a location so that the operator has a clear view of the user loading and unloading areas.
The owner shall ensure that a designated operator shall operate only one inflatable amusement attraction at a time.
Operators.
The owner shall ensure each inflatable amusement attraction:
Is under the direct control of an operator who has been trained in the operation of the assigned inflatable amusement attraction; and
Has the appropriate number of operators in the appropriate locations as provided for by the manufacturer’s specifications.
The owner shall ensure that a designated operator:
Does not operate any inflatable amusement attraction while under the influence of drugs or alcohol;
Knows the use and function of all operating controls, signal systems, and safety devices applicable to the inflatable amusement attraction;
Knows the proper user loading sequence and capacity of the particular inflatable amusement attraction being operated;
Remains stationed at the inflatable amusement attraction during operation;
Has control of the inflatable amusement attraction at all times;
Enforces applicable restrictions; and
Has no other distractions from the operator’s responsibilities.
Operator and Attendant Identification.
An owner shall issue to each operator and attendant identification that is highly visible.
The identification shall clearly indicate the following:
The title, that is, operator or attendant;
Either the name of the operator or attendant or an identification number; and
The name of the inflatable amusement attraction company.
Before issuing any identification, the owner shall train the operator and the attendant in accordance with §I of this regulation.
Operator and Attendant Training.
An owner shall comply with the requirements of ASTM F2374-22.7.8 regarding operator and attendant training;
An operator or attendant shall be trained on how to monitor the local weather including assessing the wind speeds using an anemometer or other reliable method; and

The owner of an inflatable amusement attraction shall ensure that the Inflatable Amusement Attraction Daily Inspection and Daily Pre-Opening Checklist is properly filled out and signed at the completion of the daily pre-opening program.
Daily Inspection Program.
An owner shall comply with the requirements of ASTM F2374-22.7.7, except §7.7.4.
Worn and damaged components shall be repaired or replaced immediately and before operation.

The owner of an inflatable amusement attraction shall ensure that the Inflatable Amusement Attraction Daily Inspection and Daily Pre-Opening Checklist is properly filled out and signed at the completion of the daily pre-opening program.
A copy of the Inflatable Amusement Attraction Daily Inspection and Daily Pre-Opening Checklist shall be provided to the Commissioner on request.
Injury Reporting.
Whenever a death, in-patient hospitalization, amputation, compound fracture, broken tooth, or loss of an eye occurs during the use of an inflatable amusement attraction:
The person who directly controls the operation of the inflatable amusement attraction shall immediately close the inflatable amusement attraction until it has been inspected by the Commissioner; and
The amusement owner shall report orally or in writing to the Commissioner within 24 hours:
Date and time of the incident;
Name, address, and telephone number of the injured person and parent or guardian information if the injured person is a minor;
Age of the injured person;
Name and manufacturer of the inflatable amusement attraction;
Description of the injury;
Description of events related to the incident; and
Name, address, and phone number of the inflatable amusement attraction operator and attendants.

If an owner is unaware of the condition of a member of the public injured during the operation of an inflatable amusement attraction who was transported away from the premises by an emergency response vehicle, the owner shall report verbally to the Commissioner within 24 hours the information required in §A(1)(b) of this regulation.
Injury Recording.

In addition to the reporting requirements of §A of this regulation, all owners shall maintain an injury log of all injuries to the public that occur during use of inflatable amusement attractions that require medical treatment by a physician other than one-time first aid and does not include:
An injury that does not ordinarily require medical treatment, such as a minor burn, splinter, contusion, scratch, or cut not requiring stitches; or
A diagnostic procedure, including examination and x-ray, even though provided by a physician or other licensed professional personnel.
The injury log shall contain the following information:
Company name;
Location;
Date;
Certificate number;
Type of inflatable amusement attraction;
Nature of incident;
Description of injury;
Age of injured person;
Last known condition of injured person, including date information obtained; and
Date added to log.
Each injury required under this section and §A of this regulation, shall be recorded on the injury log within 7 calendar days and updated as additional required information becomes available.
A copy of a sample injury log is available on the Department's webpage.
An owner shall maintain injury logs on a calendar year basis.
An owner shall certify the completeness and accuracy of the information contained on the injury log by January 15 of the following calendar year.
An owner shall provide access to prior years', and the portion completed for the current year, injury log to the Commissioner.
Each owner shall maintain, and make available for inspection by the Commissioner, an injury log for 5 years.
The Inflatable Amusement Attraction Daily Inspection and Daily Pre-Opening Checklist shall be:
Signed by the person performing the inspection immediately upon completion of the inspection;
Kept with the inflatable amusement attraction or at an on-site office; and
Maintained for at least 30 days.
Identification Records. An owner shall maintain a record of the name and identification number of each operator and attendant with the following information:
Inflatable amusement attraction that the operator and attendant operated; and
The date and location of operation.
Record of Training.
Each owner shall:
Maintain a record of training provided to each operator and attendant; and
Provide the Commissioner with a copy of the record of training on request.
The training record shall indicate the following for each operator and attendant:
The name or identification number;
A detailed description of the training provided; and
A list of all the inflatable amusement attractions for which the operator and attendant received training.
Written Logs.
An owner shall ensure that a written log is maintained and:
Written log requirements:
Contain any reports from riders complaining of adverse physical effects or operating inconsistencies occurring during the operation of an amusement attraction; and
Are submitted to the Commissioner when multiple reports of a particular nature may indicate a pattern are shown in the log; and

Contains weather reports that reflect review of the weather forecast prior to each set up of the inflatable amusement attraction including the date, time, location of operation, and description of weather conditions.
A copy of the written logs required by this section shall be made available to the Commissioner upon request.
An owner shall ensure that an inflatable amusement attraction is designed and manufactured in accordance with Regulation .09 of this chapter.
Upon request, an owner shall provide the Commissioner with information to document compliance with §A of this regulation.
Copies of all documents provided to the Commissioner shall be in English.
Access to and egress from all inflatable amusement attractions shall be free from all recognized hazards.
All walking surfaces including, but not limited to, loading and unloading areas, platforms, landings, stairs, and ramps shall be stable and slip-resistant.
If there is the potential for exposure to a fall distance exceeding 30 inches, the owner shall take steps to prevent such falls.

section.heading: Signage.
section.text: An owner shall comply with the signage requirements of ASTM F2374-22.7.5.4 excluding “such as, but not limited to, those listed in 7.9” in Section 7.5.4.1.

para.text: Weather-Related and Environmental Condition Restrictions.
Except as provided in §A(2) of this regulation, all manufacturer weather-related and environmental condition restrictions shall be complied with; and.

In the event of lightning, maximum sustained wind speed in excess of 15 mph, or wind gusts lasting 3 seconds or longer with wind speed of 25 mph or greater in the immediate area where the inflatable amusement attraction is operating, a tornado watch, a severe storm predicted within a 5 mile radius, fire, or other disturbance, an owner shall implement the following:
Users shall be unloaded or evacuated safely from the inflatable amusement attraction immediately; and
The inflatable amusement attraction shall be closed and secured immediately.
Illumination.
Each inflatable amusement attraction, including entrances and exits, shall be illuminated to provide adequate visibility of the surfaces used.
For the assembly and disassembly of inflatable amusement attractions, all work areas shall be illuminated to provide adequate visibility.
Flammable and Combustible Liquids.
An owner shall store and handle flammable and combustible liquids consistent with the applicable National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) standards.
An owner may not handle or fuel equipment with flammable liquids within 20 feet of an inflatable amusement attraction.
An owner shall store flammable and combustible liquids at least 75 feet from an inflatable amusement attraction or other spark-producing equipment.
Fire Extinguishers.

An owner shall provide at least one portable fire extinguisher listed and labeled with a ABC rating, as defined by the National Electrical Code, ANSI/NFPA #70, of not less than 10 pound capacity rating.
The fire extinguisher shall be located not less than 25 feet, or more than 75 feet, from any:
Flammable or combustible liquid storage; or
Portable electric generator.
Guarding.
The owner shall ensure that machinery is guarded to prevent contact with belts, fan blades, or similar moving components.
Guards shall be:
Designed to perform a specific task;
Properly secured; and
Properly maintained in a condition consistent with the intent of the guard’s design.
Before a guard is removed for maintenance purposes:
The equipment shall be locked out or tagged out; and
The guard shall be replaced before normal operation is resumed.
A guard shall be designed so that it does not pose a hazard itself.
Portable Electric Generators.
A portable electric generator is not required to be grounded if the portable electric generator:
Supplies only equipment mounted on the generator; or
Supplies power to the equipment through receptacles mounted on the generator that are connected by plug and cord and single extensions.
If a portable electric generator does not comply with §B(1) of this regulation, the owner shall ensure that a grounding electrode, such as a ground rod, is used for any fault current.
A portable electric generator shall be listed and labeled by a Nationally Recognized Testing Laboratory.
A portable electric generator may not be located:
Near user or pedestrian traffic; or
In locations that may be hazardous.
In addition to the requirements set forth in this section, an owner shall comply with the installation requirements set forth in ASTM F2374.22.7562-7.5.6.10.
Location.
Placement of the inflatable amusement attraction at the location where it is to be operated shall ensure that:
The inflatable amusement attraction is compliant with manufacturer specifications;
The inflatable amusement attraction is on a level and stable surface after consideration of slope, topography and surface type;
The inflatable amusement attraction is not placed in a public right of way and away from motor vehicle traffic; and
The inflatable amusement attraction is accessible by emergency personnel.
Tie-Downs.
Tie-downs and anchors used to support an inflatable amusement attraction may not create a tripping or impalement hazard.
The immediate area surrounding the inflatable amusement attraction shall be clear of:
Trees and shrubs, if they pose a hazard;
Free from debris; and
Other tripping hazards including, but not limited to, pot holes.
Clearances.

Except for equipment required for operation of the inflatable amusement attraction, there shall be side and overhead clearances of at least 48 inches between an inflatable amusement attraction and any other object.
Except for conductors supplying power to the device, an inflatable amusement attraction may not operate under or within 15 feet of any power lines, utilities, roads, cliffs, or bodies of water.
In addition to the requirements set forth in this section, an owner shall comply with the installation requirements set forth in ASTM F2374-22.2.16.4.
Lighting systems. Lighting systems, including fluorescent lighting systems, shall be:
In good repair;
Protected against accidental contact and breakage; and
Capable of containing the pieces of a broken light should breakage occur.

section.heading: Rules of Procedure for Hearings.
section.text: All contested case hearings before the Commissioner shall be governed by COMAR 09.01.02.

para.text: Application for Variance.
An affected owner may apply to the Commissioner for a variance from any regulation adopted under Business Regulation Article, Title 3, Annotated Code of Maryland.
An application for variance shall be in writing and shall include:
The name and address of the applicant;
Identification of the regulation from which the applicant seeks a variance;
The location, equipment, and practices involved;
A detailed statement of the applicant’s reasons for requesting a variance;
A statement of the steps the applicant has taken and will take, with specific dates when appropriate, to protect the public against the specific hazard covered by the regulation; and
Any request for a hearing.
Posting of Notice.

Immediately on filing a request for a variance pursuant to Business Regulation Article, §3-314, Annotated Code of Maryland, an applicant shall post notice of the application at the place or places where a certificate of inspection is required to be posted.
The notice shall include:
The name and address of the applicant;
The date on which the application was filed;
Identification of the regulation from which the applicant seeks a variance;
The location, equipment, and practices involved; and
Notice to the public and affected employees of their right to participate in the proceeding.
The notice shall be maintained until the commencement of the proceedings.
Order of Variance.
After investigation, on-site inspection, and such hearing as the Commissioner may direct, the Commissioner may grant a variance from a regulation under this chapter if:
It is evident that action is necessary to prevent undue hardship; or
Existing conditions prevent practical compliance and the reasonable safety of the public can, in the opinion of the Commissioner, be ensured.
An order of variance may require such special conditions as are reasonably necessary to ensure public safety.

A declaration, act, or omission of the Commissioner or the Commissioner’s authorized representative, other than a written order authorizing a variance as permitted above, may not be construed to exempt, either wholly or in part, expressly or implicitly, any owner from full compliance with the terms of any regulation under this chapter.
Modification, Revocation, and Renewal of Order of Variance.
The Commissioner may modify, revoke, or renew an order of variance in the same manner prescribed for the issuance of an order of variance.
An order of variance may not be modified or revoked unless affected parties are informed of the proposed action and afforded an opportunity to:
Submit written data, views, or arguments regarding the proposed action; and
Request a hearing on the proposal.

annotations.annotation: Business Regulation Article, §§3-204(c), 3-307—3-313 and 3-402, Annotated Code of Maryland
53:6 26-005-E (Regulations .02, .03, .09 — .22) EMERGENCY ACTION IN EFFECT 3/4/2026 — 8/31/2026
Effective date: January 21, 2013 (40:1 Md. R. 20)
Regulation .01B amended effective November 1, 2022 (49:21 Md. R. 951)
Regulation .03B amended effective November 1, 2022 (49:21 Md. R. 951)
Regulation .10 amended effective November 1, 2022 (49:21 Md. R. 951)
Chapter revised as an emergency provision effective March 4, 2026 (53:6 Md. R. 285)

para.text: Recreational.

Except for crab pots from private property, dipnets, or handlines, a person may not set any crabbing gear or catch crabs for recreational purposes on Wednesdays in the Chesapeake Bay and its tidal tributaries.
When a State or federal holiday falls on a Wednesday or Thursday, recreational crabbing is permitted on the Wednesday of that week.
An individual may not offer to sell or sell crabs that were caught for recreational purposes.
An individual may not knowingly buy or offer to buy crabs which were caught for recreational purposes.

An individual licensed to provide services as a fishing guide or to fish recreationally in the Chesapeake Bay and its tidal tributaries may possess any number of soft crabs or male peeler crabs for the purposes of fishing.
An individual licensed to catch crabs for commercial purposes:
May catch crabs for recreational purposes with recreational crabbing gear without paying the fee for recreational crabbing set forth in Regulation .09 of this chapter; and
Has the same restrictions as an individual with a recreational crabbing license when engaged in recreational crabbing activities.
Commercial — General.
A person licensed to catch crabs for sale may not set or retrieve gear or catch crabs for commercial purposes in the Chesapeake Bay and its tidal tributaries on Sundays and Mondays unless the person:
Declares intent under §C of this regulation;
Is granted an alternate day off under §D of this regulation;
Is operating as a licensee's authorized user on the vessel designated in accordance with Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland; or
Is assisting as an unlicensed crew member on another licensee's vessel.
A person who is granted an alternate day off under §D of this regulation may not set or retrieve gear or catch crabs for commercial purposes on the alternate day off.

A person licensed to catch crabs for commercial purposes, who declares under §C(1) of this regulation, may set or retrieve gear or catch crabs for commercial purposes on a Sunday or Monday of a particular week if:
A State or federal holiday falls on the Sunday or Monday, or the immediately preceding Saturday; or
The Sunday or Monday precedes a State or federal holiday that falls on a Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, or Friday of the particular week.
Mature female hard crabs shall be kept in separate bushels, lugs, or barrels from male hard crabs or peeler crabs.
Mature female hard crabs on board a vessel shall be stored in the same type of containers of similar dimensions.
Male hard crabs on board a vessel shall be stored in the same type of containers of similar dimensions.
Except as specified in §F(2) of this regulation, an individual licensed to catch crabs for commercial purposes may not catch or possess in the Chesapeake Bay and its tidal tributaries more than:
Six mature female hard crabs per container smaller than a barrel of male crabs; or
13 mature female hard crabs per barrel of male crabs.

Beginning in 2010, an individual with a limited crab harvester (LCC) license will be considered as limited crab harvester frozen license status, as specified in §F(2)(b) of this regulation, if that license is not designated as a:
Limited crab harvester active license; or
Limited crab harvester male only license.
The commercial female crab daily catch limit shall be determined by the license type based on the abundance target for spawning age crabs recommended by the Chesapeake Bay Stock Assessment Committee.

Except as provided in §B(11) of this regulation, an individual licensed to catch crabs for commercial purposes may not harvest or possess more mature female hard crabs daily than the catch limit specified by the individual's license type.

Except as provided in §B(11) of this regulation, more than two licensees' daily catch limits from 1 day may not be harvested from, carried on, or possessed on a vessel while the licensees are on board.
An individual may possess and transport crabs harvested from the waters of the Potomac River or Virginia if:
The individual is not engaged in harvesting crabs in Maryland waters while the crabs harvested in the waters of the Potomac River or Virginia are on board the vessel; and
The individual is in possession of a Virginia commercial crab harvester license or Potomac River Fisheries Commission commercial license.
A float used for holding or shedding peelers shall have a base that is a minimum of 16 square feet.
An individual commercially harvesting crabs aboard a vessel may only have crabs that are commercially harvested on board.
Closed Day Declaration of Intent.

A person licensed to catch crabs for sale and who wishes to set or retrieve gear or catch crabs on one of the required days off in §B(1) of this regulation shall declare that person's intent to set or retrieve gear or catch crabs in the Chesapeake Bay and its tidal tributaries for commercial purposes on one of the required days off as specified in §B(1) of this regulation.

A person licensed to catch crabs for sale who declares a day off under §C(1) of this regulation shall declare a Sunday or Monday day off only at the time of license renewal or issuance on forms provided by the Department.
Alternate Closed Day.

A person licensed to catch crabs for sale may apply to the Department for an alternate to the closed days specified in §B(1) of this regulation. The Department may grant a licensee's request for an alternate closed day if the licensee demonstrates the:
Imposition of substantial hardship on the licensee's exercise of religion by observing one of the closed days specified in §B(1) of this regulation; and
Absence of substantial hardship on the licensee's exercise of religion by observing the alternate closed day requested in the application.
A licensee who is granted an alternate closed day may set gear and catch crabs on the closed days specified in §B(1) of this regulation.

A person licensed to catch crabs for sale who declares a day off under §D(1) of this regulation shall declare an alternate day off only at the time of license renewal or issuance on forms provided by the Department.
Notwithstanding Natural Resources Article, §4-809, Annotated Code of Maryland, a person may not:
Catch or possess more than 6 hard crabs per container smaller than a barrel or 13 hard crabs per barrel which measure less than the minimum size specified in these regulations;
Possess hard crabs imported from another State which:
Measure less than 5 inches across the shell from tip to tip of the spike;
Do not comply with the tolerance limits of the jurisdiction of origin;
Are not accompanied by a bill of sale or bill of lading from the jurisdiction of origin; and
Were not caught in full compliance with the laws of the jurisdiction of origin;

From April 1 through July 14, catch or possess a hard crab which measures less than 5 inches across the shell from tip to tip of the spike, from the waters of the Chesapeake Bay and its tidal tributaries, except that the minimum size of crabs does not apply to mature female crabs, identified by the apron;

After July 14, catch or possess a hard crab which measures less than 5¼ inches across the shell from tip to tip of the spike from the waters of the Chesapeake Bay and its tidal tributaries, except that the minimum size of crabs does not apply to mature female crabs, identified by the apron;

Catch or possess a hard crab which measures less than 5 inches across the shell from tip to tip of the spike from the waters of Worcester County, except that the minimum size of crabs does not apply to mature female crabs, identified by the apron;
Except as provided in §E(7) of this regulation, catch or possess more than 10 peeler crabs per bushel or more than 20 per float which are:
Less than 3-1/4 inches across the shell from tip to tip of the spike during the period from April 1 through July 14; and
Less than 3-1/2 inches across the shell from tip to tip of the spike during the period from July 15 through December 15;

Catch or possess more than 10 female peeler crabs per bushel or more than 20 per float which are less than 3½ inches across the shell from tip to tip of the spike in the Chesapeake Bay and its tidal tributaries during the period from April 1, 2015 through December 15, 2015; or
Catch or possess more than one soft crab per 2 dozen soft crabs which is less than 3½ inches across the shell from tip to tip of the spike.
Limited Crab Harvester.
Latent Effort Limited Crab Harvester License.

A limited crab harvester (LCC) authorization of a tidal fish license that has no reported crab harvest between January 1, 2004 and December 15, 2008 on reports submitted to the Department by January 30, 2009, shall be determined to be a latent effort limited crab harvester license, except for those licenses specified in §F(1)(b) of this regulation.
Exceptions to Latent Effort Designation. The following are exceptions to the latent effort designation:
An LCC authorization received through the apprenticeship program;
An LCC authorization received through a permanent license transfer between August 1, 2006 and July 8, 2009;
An LCC authorization assigned to an individual who provides appropriate documentation of active military service during the period January 1, 2004 and January 30, 2009;
An LCC assigned to an individual who completed a successful female bushel limit hardship appeal in 2008; and
An LCC assigned to an individual who currently resides and has resided for at least 5 years on an island in the State that is at least 3 miles from the mainland.
Latent Effort Limited Crab Harvester Status.

An individual with a license that has been determined to be a latent effort license as described in §F(1) of this regulation is required to have declared their LCC license as a limited crab harvester — male only as described in §F(2)(b) of this regulation.
An individual with a limited crab harvester license — male only status:
Shall only harvest male crabs beginning on April 1, 2010;
May not harvest females at any time regardless of the blue crab population target level being achieved for 3 consecutive years;
Shall renew the license annually in accordance with the schedule and fees designated for a LCC authorization in Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland; and

May apply to the Department for a permanent or temporary transfer of the license through a family, business, or beneficiary transfer as described in Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland.
The latent effort license status may not be modified after declaration even if the license has been transferred to a new owner.
Conditions for using an LCC male only authorization as one of the required authorizations to upgrade to an unlimited tidal fish authorization are described in COMAR 08.02.01.05I.

Notwithstanding Natural Resources Article, §4-701, Annotated Code of Maryland, any LCC which was previously frozen due to latent license status as described in §F(1) of this regulation will not be required to pay the authorization fee for the 2012 commercial crabbing season.
Public Notice.

The Secretary may establish or modify catch limits or modify seasons for blue crabs in order to implement the Chesapeake Bay Blue Crab Fishery Management Plan by issuing a public notice on the Department's website.
The public notice shall state its effective hour and date and shall be published on the Department's website at least 48 hours in advance of the effective hour and date.

The Secretary shall make a reasonable effort to disseminate a public notice issued under this section through various other media so that an affected person has a reasonable opportunity to be informed.
A violation of the restrictions set by the Secretary in accordance with this section is a violation of this regulation.

Annotation: An amendment to Regulation .01J promulgated at 14:2 Md. R. 126 was declared invalid by the Department of Natural Resources and is not reflected in the 1987 COMAR supplement.

para.text: Definition.
In this regulation, the following term has the meaning stated.
Term Defined. “Shellfish” means live bivalves and bivalve shells.

Permit Required. A person may not import or possess within the State shellfish taken from waters outside the waters of the State for planting in the waters of the State, unless they first obtain a permit from the Department. The Department shall issue a permit if presented with proof satisfactory to the Department that the shellfish will not be harmful to Maryland shellfish.

Zebra Mussel. Except as permitted by the Secretary of Natural Resources, a person may not import into the State or possess any living life stage or reproductive products of mussels of the genus Dreissena.

section.heading: Scope.
section.text: Scope. This chapter governs Kiosks, as such term is defined herein, and the conduct of any person operating a Kiosk in the State.

para.text: In this chapter, the following terms have the meanings indicated.
Terms Defined
“Commissioner” has the meaning stated in Financial Institutions Article, §1-101, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Electronic Notice of Fraud” means any

Any electronic communication from a User to an Operator expressing a concern that a transaction the User conducted using a Virtual Currency Service provided through the Operator’s Kiosk was a Fraudulent Transaction; and
Sent to an Operator using either:
A process established on the Operator’s website for submitting a Notice of Fraud through the website; or
An email address the Operator uses for receiving Notices of Fraud.
“Fee” means any cost, charge, fee, or other expenses, however defined, collected by or on behalf of an Operator and collected from or charged to any User in connection with obtaining:
A Virtual Currency Service from a Kiosk; or
Any money transmission involving virtual currency facilitated through a Kiosk.
“Fixed Location Kiosk” means a Kiosk the Operator intends to operate at a single location for the calendar year in which the Kiosk is registered hereunder.
“Fraud” means:
Any common law fraud;
An intentional act or attempt to obtain something of value from a person through threats, willful misrepresentation, or representations made with a reckless disregard for the truth; or

Any willful false or reckless representation of a material fact, whether by words or by conduct, by false, reckless, or misleading allegations, or by concealment of that which should have been disclosed, which causes a person to act, or fail to act, to the detriment of the person’s interest.

“Fraudulent Transaction” means any transaction involving a User who obtains a Virtual Currency Service from a Kiosk in connection with the transaction if the User engaged in the transaction because of Fraud perpetrated by another person.
Kiosk.
“Kiosk” means a virtual currency kiosk
“Kiosk” includes any Fixed Location Kiosk and Mobile Kiosk.
“Mail” means first class United States mail, postage prepaid.
“Mobile Kiosk” means a Kiosk the Operator intends to operate at more than one location for the calendar year in which the Kiosk is registered.
“Money Transmission” has the meaning stated in Financial Institutions Article, §12-401, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“NMLS” has the meaning stated in Financial Institutions Article, §1-101, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Non-Electronic Notice of Fraud” means:

Any verbal or written communication from a User to an Operator which expresses a concern that the transaction the User conducted with a Virtual Currency Service provided through the Operator’s Kiosk was a Fraudulent Transaction; and
Sent to an Operator in any manner that does not constitute an Electronic Notice of Fraud.
“Notice of Fraud” means any:
Electronic Notice of Fraud; and
Non-electronic Notice of Fraud.
“Operator” means a Virtual Currency Kiosk Operator.
User.
“User” means any person using a Kiosk located in the State to obtain a Virtual Currency Service.
“User” includes a person who authorizes a third party to obtain a Virtual Currency Service using a Kiosk on behalf of the User.
“Receipt” means the receipt an Operator must dispense or display to a User under Financial Institutions Article §12-1204, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Registration Fee” means any fee imposed by the Commissioner to:
Register as an Operator; or
Register a Kiosk.
“State” means the state of Maryland.
“Virtual Currency” has the meaning stated in Financial Institutions Article, §12-1201, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Virtual Currency Address” means an alphanumeric identifier representing a destination for a virtual currency transfer that is associated with a virtual currency wallet.
“Virtual Currency Kiosk” has the meaning stated in Financial Institutions Article, §12-1201, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Virtual Currency Kiosk Operator” has the meaning stated in Financial Institutions Article, §12-1201, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Virtual Currency Service” has the meaning stated in Financial Institutions Article, §12-1201, Annotated Code of Maryland.
“Virtual Currency Wallet” means a software application or other mechanism providing a means for holding, storing and transferring virtual currency.
Registration of Operator.
A person may not operate a Kiosk in the State after January 1, 2026, unless registered as an Operator with the Commissioner.
A person shall register through NMLS with the Commissioner as an Operator:
On or before January 1, 2026, if operating a Kiosk in the State on or before that date; or
Prior to operating a Kiosk in the State after January 1, 2026.
A person registering as an Operator shall pay:
The Commissioner a non-refundable Registration Fee of $2,000; and
Any cost or fee required by NMLS.
A person registering as an Operator shall provide through NMLS:
The legal name of the Operator and any trade name used by the Operator in accordance with Financial Institutions Article §2-121, Annotated Code of Maryland;
The address of the operator’s principal executive office;
The names of any persons directly or indirectly holding an ownership share of 10 percent or more of the operator;
A flow of funds for all Virtual Currency Services the Operator will offer through its Kiosks; and
Any other information the Commissioner requests.
Registration of Kiosks.
An Operator may not operate a Kiosk in the State on or after January 1, 2026, unless the Operator has registered that Kiosk with the Commissioner.
A person shall register each Kiosk operated in the State through NMLS with the Commissioner:
On or before January 1, 2026, if operating that Kiosk in the State on or before that date; or
Prior to operating that Kiosk in the State after January 1, 2026.
An Operator registering a Kiosk shall pay:
The Commissioner a non-refundable Registration Fee of $200 for each registered Kiosk; and
Any fee required by NMLS.
An Operator registering a Kiosk shall provide through NMLS:
Unique identifying information for the Kiosk, such as the manufacturer name, model number, serial number, or asset tag;
If the Kiosk is a Fixed Location Kiosk, the address at which the Kiosk will be operated;
If the Kiosk is a Mobile Kiosk:
The address of each location at which the Operator expects to operate the Kiosk for more than 30 calendar days; and
The geographic area encompassing the locations at which the Operator expects to operate the Kiosk for 30 calendar days or less;
A description of all services to be provided by the Kiosk; and
Any other information the Commissioner requests.
An Operator shall designate a Kiosk intended for use as a Fixed Location Kiosk as a Fixed Location Kiosk through NMLS in accordance with the process the Commissioner requires.
An Operator shall designate a Kiosk intended for use as a Mobile Kiosk as a Mobile Kiosk through NMLS in accordance with the process the Commissioner requires.
An Operator may only operate a Kiosk at the address or, if a Mobile Kiosk, in the geographic area listed for that Kiosk in NMLS.
An Operator may change the designation of a Kiosk as either a Mobile Kiosk or a Fixed Location Kiosk through NMLS by:
Changing the designation through the process the Commissioner requires;
Paying any required fee; and
Updating the Kiosk’s location information.
Contemporaneously with changing a Kiosk’s location, an Operator must update information in NMLS to accurately reflect the new location.
Term of Registrations and Renewals.
The term of any registration under this regulation shall:
Begin the day the Operator completes the registration in NMLS; and
Expire on December 31 of the year:
The Operator completes the registration, if completed before November 1; or
Succeeding the year the Operator completes the registration, if completed between November 1 and December 31.
On or after November 1 of each year, registration as an Operator and for each individual Kiosk under this regulation may be renewed for a 1-year term if the Operator:
Is otherwise entitled to be registered;
Completes the renewal process established through NMLS;
Provides through NMLS any information the Commissioner requests as part of the renewal;
Submits through NMLS the annual report required by Regulation .08 of this chapter;
Pays any fees imposed by NMLS;
Pays the Commissioner a non-refundable Registration Fee of $2,000 to register as an Operator; and
Pays the Commissioner a non-refundable Registration Fee of $200 for each Kiosk the Operator intends to operate in the State.
A person may not renew registration for a Kiosk unless the person has renewed its registration as an Operator.
All Registration Fees shall be paid to the Commissioner through NMLS.
Money Transmission.

Registration as an Operator does not exempt a person from licensure under Financial Institutions Article, Title 12, Subtitle 4, Annotated Code of Maryland, if one or more Kiosks operated by the Operator facilitate money transmission.

Possession of a license, or exemption from licensure, under Financial Institutions Article, Title 12, Subtitle 4, Annotated Code of Maryland, does not exempt a person from registration under Financial Institutions Article, Title 12, Subtitle 12, Annotated Code of Maryland, if that person operates a Kiosk offering virtual currency services.

A Kiosk shall be deemed to facilitate money transmission if it is capable of facilitating money transmission, regardless of whether the Operator purports to offer money transmission services through that kiosk, unless the Operator disables this function and prevents such facilitation.
An Operator offering both Virtual Currency Services and money transmission through a Kiosk shall fully comply with all applicable provisions of:
Financial Institutions Article, Title 12, Subtitle 4, Annotated Code of Maryland, and all regulations issued thereunder; and
Financial Institutions Article, Title 12, Subtitle 12, Annotated Code of Maryland, and all regulations issued thereunder.
Other Services.
An Operator may only offer the following services through a Kiosk:
Virtual Currency Services;
Money transmission, provided the Operator complies with the licensing provisions of Financial Institutions Article, Title 12, Subtitle 4, Annotated Code of Maryland; and
Any other service State law expressly authorizes an operator to offer through a Kiosk.
Any service offered through a Kiosk must comply with all State law relating to that service, including all applicable licensing or registration requirements.
Scope.
This regulation governs the procedure by which a User may request a Fee Refund on a transaction the User alleges is a Fraudulent Transaction.
A User’s rights under this regulation are in addition to any rights a User may have under any applicable law.
Definitions.
In this regulation, the following terms have the meanings indicated.
Terms Defined.
“Fee Refund” means a refund under this regulation of the Fee charged to that User.
“Refund Claim” means a User’s claim for a Fee Refund.
“Refund Denial” means an Operator’s denial of a Refund Claim.
“Written Confirmation” means a written confirmation of a verbally given Notice of Fraud.
Notice of Fraud by User.
An Operator shall treat any Notice of Fraud as a Refund Claim.
A User must provide a Notice of Fraud to an Operator no more than 90 calendar days after the alleged Fraudulent Transaction.
A Notice of Fraud is sufficient for this regulation if it:
Includes the User’s name;
Provides a copy of the Receipt or otherwise provides information sufficient to identify the Virtual Currency Services the User obtained through the Operator’s Kiosk; and
Contains a brief description of why the User believes the transaction was a Fraudulent Transaction.
An Operator may not engage in any act or subterfuge to make it difficult for a User to provide a Notice of Fraud.
Place of Notice.
An Operator must offer a User:
At least one method to submit an Electronic Notice of Fraud; and
At least one method to submit a Non-electronic Notice of Fraud.
The Operator shall conspicuously include information on its website and in any promotional materials explaining how a User may submit:
An Electronic Notice of Fraud; and
A Non-electronic Notice of Fraud.
An Operator may require the User to submit a Non-electronic Notice of Fraud only to a telephone number or address disclosed on the Receipt, provided that the Operator:
Has reasonable procedures to redirect a User to the specified telephone number or address if the User attempts to provide a Non-electronic Notice of Fraud by other means;

Has reasonable procedures to route any written Non-electronic Notice of Fraud the Operator receives at any other address to the department or personnel responsible for investigating and resolving a Refund Claim; and
Conspicuously displays the designated telephone number or address for a User to provide a Non-electronic Notice of Fraud on any website the Operator maintains.
If a User provides a written Notice of Fraud to an address other than the address designated by the Operator and the Operator receives such Notice of Fraud:
The Operator must process the Notice of Fraud as a Refund Claim in accordance with this regulation; and
The time frame for investigating and resolving the Refund Claim shall begin 5 business days after the Operator’s receipt of the Notice of Fraud at the incorrect address.
Written Confirmation.
An Operator may require a User to provide Written Confirmation of a verbal Non-electronic Notice of Fraud within 30 calendar days of the verbal Non-electronic Notice of Fraud.
An Operator requiring a Written Confirmation under this Section shall:
At the time of the verbal Non-electronic Notice of Fraud:
Inform the User that the Operator must receive a Written Confirmation; and
Inform the User that the Operator will mail the User a notice concerning the Written Confirmation; and
Within 3 business days of the verbal Non-electronic Notice of Fraud, mail the User written notice stating:
The Operator requires a Written Confirmation;
A specific date at least 30 calendar days after the date of the notice by which the User must submit the Written Confirmation;
The address to send the Written Confirmation; and
The User’s failure to timely provide a Written Confirmation may result in a Refund Denial.
An Operator may not require the Written Confirmation to include information inconsistent with the definition of a Non-electronic Notice of Fraud.
A User who verbally provides a Non-electronic Notice of Fraud within the time frame established by §E(2) of this regulation has timely submitted a Notice of Fraud.
If a User fails to provide the Written Confirmation within 30 calendar days of the Operator’s full compliance with §E(2) of this regulation:
The Operator may mail written notice to the User:
Informing the User that the Operator has not received the Written Confirmation;
Requiring the User to provide the Written Confirmation within 30 calendar days of the notice; and
Informing the User the Operator may deny the Refund Claim if the User fails to provide the Written Confirmation within 30 calendar days of the notice.
A User’s failure to provide a timely Written Confirmation following the Operator’s compliance with §E(5)(a) of this regulation constitutes grounds to issue a Refund Denial.
Investigation and Resolution.
Except as otherwise expressly set forth in this regulation, an Operator shall have 30 calendar days from receipt of a Notice of Fraud to investigate and resolve the Refund Claim.

If the Operator requested a Written Confirmation in compliance with §E of this regulation, the Operator shall have 30 calendar days from receipt of the Written Confirmation to investigate and resolve the Refund Claim.
In connection with its investigation of a Refund Claim, an Operator may:
Inquire if the User filed a police report;
Inquire if the User possesses any documents or communications relating to the alleged Fraudulent Transaction; and
Request copies of any police report filed or documents possessed by the User and relating to the alleged Fraudulent Transaction.
In connection with its investigation of a Refund Claim, an Operator may not:
Require the filing of a police report;
Require the User to provide copies of a filed police report or documents relating to the alleged Fraudulent Transaction;
Imply that a failure to file a police report, provide copies of a filed police report, or provide copies of documents relating to the alleged Fraudulent Transaction will impact the Refund Claim;

Issue a Refund Denial based on a failure to file a police report, failure to provide copies of a filed police report, or failure to provide copies of documents relating to the to the alleged Fraudulent Transaction; or
Charge any cost, fee, or other amount to investigate and resolve a Refund Claim.
A User shall reasonably cooperate with the Operator’s investigation of a Refund Claim by answering questions concerning the Fraudulent Transaction.
Within 3 business days of completing its investigation, an Operator shall resolve a Refund Claim by mailing the User either:
A Refund Denial; or
A statement granting the Refund Claim.
A Refund Denial must:
Generally describe the Operator’s investigative process;
List any facts the Operator relied on for the Refund Denial;
Describe any other factors the Operator relied on for the Refund Denial;
Inform the User of the right to file a complaint regarding the Refund Denial with the Commissioner; and
Provide the User with a link or other information needed to reach the Commissioner.
An Operator may not issue a Refund Denial based on any of the following:
User’s negligence;
User’s failure to consider or act upon warnings and disclosures provided by the Operator;
User’s responses to questions posed by the Operator when the User obtained the Virtual Currency Services; or
User submission of a Notice of Fraud for a prior transaction.
A Fee Refund shall be issued promptly in United States currency.
A User receiving a Refund Denial may file a complaint with the Commissioner.
A complaint concerning a Refund Denial must be filed with the Commissioner within 3 years of the User’s receipt of the Refund Denial.
An Operator shall maintain restrictions that prevent more than one User of the Operator from using the same virtual currency wallet.
An Operator shall have the ability to prevent designated virtual currency wallets from being used at any Kiosk of the Operator.

An Operator shall use an established third party that specializes in performing blockchain analyses to preemptively perform such analyses to identify and prevent high risk or sanctioned virtual currency wallets from being used by Users at Kiosks of the Operator.
An Operator shall establish, in the Operator’s policies and procedures, a risk-based method of monitoring Users on a post-transaction basis.
An Operator shall designate and employ a chief compliance officer.

An Operator shall provide all required notices and communications to a User under this chapter using information the Operator collects from the User under Financial Institutions Article, §12-1204 (c), Annotated Code of Maryland.
This regulation does not apply to:
The Receipt; and
Any notices or communications this chapter requires the Operator to:
Physically place on the Kiosk; or
Display on a Kiosk screen.
An Operator shall provide reasonable procedures for a User to securely change contact information the User previously supplied to the Operator.
An Operator shall provide reasonable procedures for a User to select the User’s preferred method of communication.
The User’s selection of a preferred method of communication shall not relieve an Operator of any duty to provide information in a manner expressly required by this chapter.
Each Kiosk operated in the State by an Operator shall comply with Financial Institutions Article, §§1-402 (b), (c) and 1-402.1, Annotated Code of Maryland for each Kiosk it operates.
For the purposes of this regulation only, the Operator shall treat the Kiosk as an automated teller machine.
The exceptions contained in Financial Institutions Article, §§1-402 (a), Annotated Code of Maryland do not apply to a Kiosk.
An Operator shall evaluate the safety of each Kiosk, regardless of location.

The required safety evaluation need not consider the factors identified in Financial Institutions Article, §§1-402(b)(2), Annotated Code of Maryland, for a Kiosk located in a building meeting the requirements of Financial Institutions Article, §§1-402 (a)(2)(i), Annotated Code of Maryland.

Each Kiosk operated in the State shall conspicuously display signage stating: “This Kiosk offers services relating to virtual currencies. The Maryland Commissioner of Financial Regulation will accept all questions or complaints regarding this Kiosk, operated by (name of Operator and NMLS unique identifier) at (address of Commissioner), phone (toll-free phone number of the Commissioner), or https://labor.maryland.gov/finance/ . You may confirm registration of this Kiosk with the Maryland Commissioner of Financial Regulation at (NMLS consumer access website).”
All required on-screen notices and disclosures under Financial Institutions Article §12-1201 et. seq., Annotated Code of Maryland, and this chapter shall:
Remain on the Kiosk screen for a minimum of:
Four seconds if the notice or disclosure contains 12 or less words; or
One second for every 3 words, or fractions thereof, contained in the notice or disclosure; and
Require User action to advance to another screen.

For the purposes of calculating the time period a notice or disclosure must remain on a screen pursuant to Part A of this regulation, the words contained in a notice or disclosure include words that require scrolling to view.
In addition to information required by Financial Institutions Article, §12-1204, Annotated Code of Maryland, a Receipt shall include:

The name of, and contact information for, the Operator, including, but not limited to, the Operator's business address and a customer service telephone number established by the Operator to answer questions and register complaints;
The Operator’s NMLS unique identifier;
The User’s name;
The date of the transaction reflected in the Receipt;
The amount of such virtual currency transaction expressed in United States currency;
The exchange rate for the transaction, if applicable;
The virtual currency address for that virtual currency transaction;
The full unique transaction hash or identification number associated with the transaction;
The public virtual currency address of the User;
Any fee charged by the Operator;
Any tax collected by the Operator for such virtual currency transaction;
If the transaction reflected in the Receipt involves money transmission, the recipient’s name and virtual currency address;
A statement that the User may request a Fee Refund of any Fee charged in connection with a Fraudulent Transaction by submitting a Notice of Fraud;

A statement explaining how a User may submit a Notice of Fraud, including any address or phone number the Operator has designated pursuant to Regulation .03 of this chapter for submitting a Notice of Fraud; and

A statement the Maryland Commissioner of Financial Regulation will accept all questions or complaints regarding the Kiosk, at (address of Commissioner), phone (toll-free phone number of the Commissioner), or https://labor.maryland.gov/finance/.
The Receipt shall be provided in English and in the any other language principally used by the Operator to advertise its services or transact business on the Kiosk.
The Kiosk shall offer the User the ability to elect to receive the Receipt electronically at the time of the transaction;
If the User does not elect electronic delivery of the Receipt, the Receipt shall be delivered to the User in retainable form at the time of the transaction.
An Operator shall maintain all transactional data required for a Receipt for a period of 7 years from the date of the transaction.

A Receipt that satisfies the requirements of this Regulation shall be deemed to satisfy the requirements contained in COMAR 09.03.14.16C for a virtual currency transaction conducted on a Kiosk that involves money transmission.
Compliance with COMAR 09.03.14.16C does not relieve an Operator of any additional requirements imposed by this regulation.
An Operator shall prepare an annual report listing the following information for each Kiosk it maintains:
Information sufficient to identify that Kiosk;
The total number of transactions and the total dollar amount of transactions processed by that Kiosk;
If the Kiosk was not operating for the full 12 months covered by the annual report, information sufficient to determine the timeframe in which the Operator operated that Kiosk in the State;
The number of Notices of Fraud received for that Kiosk;
If the Kiosk is a Mobile Kiosk, the location of the Mobile Kiosk at the time of the transaction described in each Notice of Fraud;
The number and dollar amount of Refund Denials issued for that Kiosk;
The number and dollar amount of Fee Refunds issued for that Kiosk; and
The dollar amount of Fees subject to Notices of Fraud for that Kiosk.
The Annual Report required by this regulation:
Should include information with respect to each Kiosk for the lesser of:
The preceding 12 months; or
The timeframe in which that Kiosk offered Virtual Currency Services in the State; and
May run for any 12 consecutive monthly period ending no earlier than September 1 of the current calendar year.
The Operator shall submit this annual report to the Commissioner through NMLS:
At the time the Operator renews its Operator registration in accordance with Regulation .03 of this chapter; or
On or before December 31st of the current calendar year if the Operator does not intend to renew its Operator registration in accordance with Regulation .03 of this chapter.

annotations.annotation: Financial Institutions Article, §§2-105.1, and 12-1209, Annotated Code of Maryland
Effective date:

section.heading: Incorporation by Reference.
section.text: The 2024 International Swimming Pool and Spa Code (ISPSC), International Code Council, is incorporated by reference.

para.text: In Section 305.1, replace the word “not” with “also” to read “The provisions of this section shall apply to the design of barriers for restricting entry into areas having pools and spas. Where spas or hot tubs are equipped with a lockable safety cover complying with ASTM F1346 and swimming pools are equipped with a powered safety cover that complies with ASTM F1346, the areas where those spas, hot tubs or pools are located shall also be required to comply with Sections 305.2 through 305.7”.
Rename Section 305.2 to “Swimming pools and spas” and replace the text with the following:

A public swimming pool or spa shall be surrounded by a barrier that complies with Sections 305.2.1 through 305.7, except that the top of the barrier where measured on the side of the barrier that faces away from the swimming pool or spa shall be not less than:
72 inches (1828mm) above grade for a Class A, B, C that is considered an apartment complex, housing subdivision, or mobile home park with more than ten units, or D swimming pool or spa;
60 inches (1524mm) above grade for a Class C that is considered a hotel, motel, or condominium or E swimming pool or spa; and
36 inches (914.4mm) above grade for a wading pool.
Non-public swimming pools shall be surrounded by a barrier that complies with Sections 305.2.1 through 305.7.
Except as provided in §B(4) of this regulation, Sections 305.2.4 - 305.2.5.1 shall only apply to non-public pools.

Exception: For non-public spas or hot tubs equipped with a lockable safety cover complying with ASTM F1346, the areas where those spas or hot tubs are located shall not be required to comply with Sections 305.2.1 through 305.7.

In Section 307.1.5, where “International Building Code” appears, substitute “COMAR 09.12.53 Maryland Accessibility Code” and, where “ICC A117.1” appears, substitute “COMAR 09.12.53 Maryland Accessibility Code”.
Replace Section 314.7 with the following:
“314.7 Emergency shutoff switch.
An emergency shutoff switch shall be provided to disconnect power to the recirculation systems.

An emergency shutoff switch shall be provided to disconnect power to feature pump systems, jet system pumps, and air blowers. These shutoff switches shall be: provided with access; located within sight of the pool or spa; and located not less than 5 feet (1524 mm) horizontally from the inside walls of the pool or spa.
Paragraphs (a) and (b) of this Section do not apply to onground storable pools, permanent inground residential swimming pools, residential spas and residential water features.”
In Section 325.7.1, where “International Fire Code” appears, substitute “COMAR 29.06.01 Maryland State Fire Prevention Code.”
Replace Section 410.1 “Toilet facilities” with the following:

“410.1 “Toilet facilities". Class A, B, and C (if all units are not within 500 feet) pools shall be provided with toilet facilities having the required number of plumbing fixtures in accordance with the International Building Code or the International Plumbing Code.”
Add to Section 609.2.1 “Exception: This requirement shall not apply to Class C semipublic pools (if all units are within 500 feet).”
